interactions.summary: Jiang continues the 2026 world-war map: a U.S.-China bargain opens space for Chinese investment in Venezuela and commodity security, Japan-China conflict rises over Taiwan and Malacca, U.S.-Russia naval conflict escalates, China may finance Russian blue-water capacity, and 2026-2027 set up Iran as the decisive flashpoint after China is negotiated away.

speaker_notes.note: Continuous Jiang monologue; no substantive interviewer turn in this packet.

claims.claim: Jiang expects a U.S.-China grand bargain in which Chinese firms, not American majors, modernize Venezuela's oil industry because China needs overseas oil and Middle East supply is vulnerable in an Iran war.
Jiang predicts a major China-Japan conflict this year because both depend on trade and Middle East oil routed through Malacca, making Taiwan strategically vital for embargoing the other side.

Jiang predicts more U.S.-Russia conflict at sea because the United States is seizing or boarding shadow oil tankers, which will force Russia to challenge U.S. naval power and build a blue-water navy likely financed by China.
Jiang says 2026 is when conflicts accelerate and 2027 will be worse because he expects the United States to launch an invasion of Iran after reaching a bargain with China.

Jiang expects Iran destabilization in 2026 through protests, border insurgent groups, airstrikes, CIA financing, Mossad agents, blockade, sabotage, infiltration, and narratives of imminent regime collapse.

signature_moments.moment: China's shipbuilding capacity turns any U.S.-Russia sea fight into a naval war of attrition America cannot comfortably sustain.
signature_moments.source_phrase: one ship for every 250 ships that China produces
signature_moments.why_it_matters: It gives Jiang's ocean-front forecast an industrial base rather than a purely military image.

signature_moments.moment: Iran is not a side issue but the hinge of American hegemony, Belt and Road, and Russia's southern flank.
signature_moments.source_phrase: iran is crucial to the maintenance of american hegemony
signature_moments.why_it_matters: This explains why Jiang moves from U.S.-China bargaining to a predicted Iran invasion.

glossary_terms.definition_or_usage: A navy capable of challenging the United States in oceanic warfare, which Jiang says Russia will need and China may finance.
China's trade/connectivity project, which Jiang says would be dead if Iran is blockaded and U.S. hegemony controls trade access.
Tankers moving Russian or Chinese oil that Jiang says the United States is boarding or seizing, creating naval conflict.

chronology_notes.note: Jiang's dated forecast: 2026 accelerates China-U.S., China-Japan, U.S.-Russia, and Iran destabilization dynamics; he expects 2027 to be worse because of a likely U.S. invasion of Iran after China is bargained away.

uncertainty_notes.note: The Japanese prime minister name is ASR-rendered as 'taki' and should be verified before public mention.
ASR renders Mossad as 'wassad' in this section; semantic pass normalizes the intended entity only in summary-level wording.
